blocks.text: peel after reading.

The process of getting to know someone deeply - in the truest sense of the word - 
[b]I think is similar to pulling back the cuticle of a finger.[/b]
You meet [i]someone[/i] and take them, almost carried by the air, to a secret place. It calls to you from afar, and when you visit, [b]it summons you[/b]: you look at their [i]ring finger[/i].
At first it's curious; you feel like pulling just for the simple satisfaction of learning something about another person so you can feel [b]human[/b] for a bit.
What could go wrong? You've taken a liking to the person, and you have to practice empathy… [b]Right[/b]?

You see the indentation of a tiny [b]crease[/b] - the smaller it is, the more frustrating it is that you can't pull on it - scraping a bit, you barely manage to [nopeel][i]grip it with your nails[/i].[/nopeel]
You hear a noise. [b]It stops you dead in your tracks.[/b]

They've told you something. Not something trivial; it's something [b]important[/b]. Someone they [i]met[/i], [i]broke up[/i], [i]died[/i]. Something they [i]lost[/i], [i]discovered[/i], had to [i]give up[/i].
It's not hard to hear; it rolled off their tongue easily - but that sentence [b]hung in the air[/b] as if it were [color=#778899]lead[/color].
You can't help but ask, as if questioning the silence: [b]Why?[/b] What went wrong? What could they have foreseen? - there's usually not much more to it.
[i]It's just life.[/i]
And then, you start [b]pulling[/b].
It's a look you hadn't seen before, somewhere between [i]confidence[/i] and [i]regret[/i], some kind of [color=#8a7a70]dirty melancholy[/color]. More is going to flow out; I can see it.
Turns out that part of their life has disappeared, or wasn't there to begin with. They didn't have, couldn't, or didn't know. [color=#784830][b]They suffered and endured[/b].[/color]
You take a moment. [b]One deep breath.[/b]
Then, [b]you continue the inertia[/b] - you peel away bit by bit, moving down the side of the nail, and each hairstrand of skin is added to the pile.
[b]They say something that makes your blood run cold.[/b] The world is [color=#b74040][b]cruel[/b][/color].
Suddenly, the dish is no longer an appetizer, but a [b]generous[/b] main course. You feel your stomach growl [i]cold[/i], [i]broken[/i], [i]distant[/i] moans.
Your eyes begin to shift to the [b]right[/b]; the mind sparks from [color=#b77713]idea[/color] to [color=#b77713]memory[/color], and without opening your mouth, your throat has already spit it.
[b]You confessed something.[/b]
It's [b]a whole lot worse[/b] than what they said. [i]Did I fuck up?[/i]
You lower your gaze - you see your hand and discover, terrified, that [b]a gash has just opened on your index finger[/b].
They start [b]to pull[/b] -
A tear wells up, you pretend you don't care and [b]vomit out linked letters[/b] until your body bears a new hole, cleaner and smoother than marble.
They open a question and without even finishing it, you've already answered it wholly with a [b]hydraulically[/b] propelled bile that makes [i]a hole on the wall[/i].
Another, and another, and another. You spit out a [b]lung[/b], an [b]eye[/b] pops out, you lose your balance.
[b]Everything slows down again.[/b]
You look at each other, and suddenly you're no longer [i]people[/i].
[b]You're slices of memories.[/b]
You know you're not going to do any kind of small talk.
You remain stoic, spitting at each other, but now [b]the two of us at once[/b].
You have a lot in common; otherwise, it wouldn't have come to this. Empathy grows into a clumsy [i]intimacy[/i]; the air is thick with sweat and a hint of copper.
Suddenly you have fewer clothes on; you've drawn closer, and fluids fly through the air as you exchange them with mouths that open and close on their own in high-frequency cycles.

There are external factors like commitments, bad memories, or emotional obstacles, but at this point the only ones that exist are, if anything, avoiding mucosal contact for fear of breaking something in your life for no good reason.

However, you're already [censor][b]touching[/b] each other.[/censor] [censor]Caressing each other's hands, even.[/censor] Depending on the situation, this could be a problem, but there's little left that can stop this now.

The conversation flows so fast and escalates so dizzyingly that we can barely process the number of needles stuck in our respective chests - [i]acupuncture[/i] that deactivated the nervous system of the extremities, secondary organs and the left hemisphere.

At this point you're not talking to each other; you're in a deep hug while tumbling down a hill, spinning on your own axes with your hearts - which long ago melted into an amorphous mass of tendons, valves, and fibers - acting as the anchor of your frenetic rotation.

The outside no longer matters; it scrapes and stumbles, spinning several times per second against the rough asphalt of that park, coming loose as a raw tangle full of dirt and pus. Eventually, the surface - increasingly spherical - slowly was becoming pure,  smooth, raw flesh.
We became. [b]Raw.[/b] [color=#b74040][b]Flesh.[/b][/color]
Questions, questions, questions,
questions, questions,
QUESTIONS, QUESTIONS,
QUESTIONS, QUESTIONS,
QUESTIONS, QUESTIONS, QUESTIONS.

Why did it have to be this way? I didn't need to meet someone like you. But now I want more. [color=#b74040][b]I need more.[/b][/color] We start pressing our faces together while grabbing the parts of our body that still remains.

[color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color], [color=#b74040]I LOVE YOU[/color].
Suddenly, the hill ends. The rhythm slows down, there isn't much left to say, and everything your body could physically feel, you felt at least a dozen times. [b]You are now raw flesh[/b].
And I'll say more: nothing will ever be able to tear you apart even if you try, and few people will understand what happened, no matter how hard you try to explain.
[i]Snuggled up, sitting on something floating, asleep in the moonlight.[/i]
You tell each other it was incredible, that you've never connected with someone like that before, and that you'll need some time to process it all. That you'll see each other [i]next time[/i].
Confidant? Future travel companion? A possible something? A possible partner? A possible marriage? A possible life? A possible -
[b]We have to stop talking.[/b]
Gustavo Chico, 2026.
